title: Feature 7: chat_resync MCP Tool
description: Add moe.chat_resync MCP tool that clears worker chatCursors and returns full message history. 1 new file + registration + docs.
definitionOfDone: Tool accepts workerId (required), channel (optional), limit (optional) parameters
Clears worker chatCursors (all or specified channel)
Returns full message history from beginning
Updates cursors to latest after resync
npm run build succeeds in packages/moe-daemon

implementationPlan.description:
Create new file `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatResync.ts` implementing `moe.chat_resync` tool.

Tool name: `moe.chat_resync`
Description: 'Clear chat cursors and return full message history for resync. Useful when a worker loses context or wants to re-read all messages.'

Input schema:
- `workerId` (string, required) — worker whose cursors to reset
- `channel` (string, optional) — specific channel to resync; omit for all channels
- `limit` (number, optional, default 50, max 200) — max messages to return per channel

Handler logic:
1. Validate `workerId` is non-empty string. Get worker via `state.getWorker(workerId)` — if not found, throw `invalidInput('workerId', 'worker not found')`.
2. Clear cursors: if `channel` specified, delete just `worker.chatCursors[channel]`; otherwise set `chatCursors` to `{}`.
3. Call `state.updateWorker(workerId, { chatCursors: clearedCursors })`.
4. Fetch messages: if `channel` specified, call `state.getMessages(channel, { limit })`. If no channel, loop all `state.getChannels()`, call `getMessages(ch.id, { limit })` for each, merge + sort by timestamp, slice to `limit`.
5. Update cursors to latest: for each message's channel, set `chatCursors[msg.channel] = msg.id` (last per channel). Call `state.updateWorker(workerId, { chatCursors: newCursors })`.
6. Return `{ success: true, messagesCount: messages.length, messages, cursorsReset: true }`.

Follow same patterns as `chatRead.ts` — import `ToolDefinition` from `./index.js`, `StateManager` from `../state/StateManager.js`, `ChatMessage` from `../types/schema.js`, `invalidInput` from `../util/errors.js`. Export function `chatResyncTool(_state: StateManager): ToolDefinition`.

Error handling: wrap handler body in try/catch, rethrow invalidInput errors, catch unexpected errors with descriptive message.
implementationPlan.note: Created chatResync.ts following chatRead.ts patterns. Clears cursors, fetches full history, updates cursors to latest.

implementationPlan.description:
Register `chatResyncTool` in `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/index.ts`.

1. Add import at line 42 (after chatWaitTool import): `import { chatResyncTool } from './chatResync.js';`
2. Add `chatResyncTool(state)` to the returned array (after `chatWaitTool(state)` at ~line 89).
implementationPlan.note: Registered chatResyncTool import and added to getTools() array.

implementationPlan.description:
Add `moe.chat_resync` tool spec to `docs/MCP_SERVER.md` in the Chat Tools section (after `moe.chat_wait` doc, around line 870).

Document:
```
### `moe.chat_resync`
Clear chat cursors and return full message history. Use when a worker loses context or wants to re-read all messages.

**Parameters:**
| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| workerId | string | Yes | Worker whose cursors to reset |
| channel | string | No | Specific channel to resync (omit for all) |
| limit | number | No | Max messages per channel (default 50, max 200) |

**Returns:** `{ success, messagesCount, messages[], cursorsReset }`
```
implementationPlan.note: Added moe.chat_resync tool spec to docs after moe.chat_wait section.

implementationPlan.description: Test: run `cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build` to verify TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors.
implementationPlan.note: Daemon compiles cleanly with no errors.

comments.author: architect
comments.content: This is daemon-only (no UI), so no JetBrains/VS Code ordering needed. Just ensure npm run build passes.
